description: Capture proof of pickup or drop-off for an order or specific waypoint/entity, supporting digital signature, photo upload, and QR code scan as verification methods.

actors.name: Driver
actors.description: Captures proof at the point of pickup or delivery via the driver app.

actors.name: Platform
actors.description: Validates and stores proof records; gates order completion when pod_required is true.

fields.name: order_id
fields.label: The order this proof is associated with.

fields.name: subject_id
fields.label: Optional waypoint or entity identifier this proof is for (omit for order-level proof).

fields.name: photo_file
fields.label: Photo file uploaded as proof (for pod_method=photo).

fields.name: qr_code
fields.label: UUID or code string from the QR scan (for pod_method=qr_scan).

fields.name: remarks
fields.label: Optional driver comments about the proof capture.

fields.name: raw_data
fields.label: Raw input data accompanying the proof (e.g., scan metadata).

fields.name: data
fields.label: Structured proof data, used for QR scan content or additional metadata.

fields.name: file_url
fields.label: URL of the uploaded signature or photo file in cloud storage.

fields.name: proof_id
fields.label: Public identifier of the created proof record.

rules.rule_01: Proof is only required when pod_required is set to true on the order.
rules.rule_02: If pod_required is true, the order completion activity is blocked until proof is captured.
rules.rule_03: A proof record can be captured at the order level or for a specific waypoint or entity within the order.
rules.rule_04: Signature capture uploads the signature as a file to cloud storage; the file URL is stored on the proof record.
rules.rule_05: QR scan proof validates that the scanned UUID matches the UUID of the subject (waypoint, entity, or order).
rules.rule_06: Photo proof stores the uploaded file URL in cloud storage.
rules.rule_07: Multiple proof records can exist for a single order (e.g., one per waypoint in a multi-stop trip).
rules.rule_08: Proof records are immutable once created.

given: driver submits a base64-encoded signature image

then.description: Signature image is stored in cloud storage and a file record is created.
Proof record is created linking the order, subject, and stored signature file.

signature_captured.result: Signature proof is stored and linked to the order or waypoint. Order can now proceed to completion.

then.description: Photo is uploaded to cloud storage.
Proof record is created linking the order and stored photo.

photo_captured.result: Photo proof is stored and linked to the order.

given: scanned code matches the UUID of the target subject (order, waypoint, or entity)

then.description: Verification proof is created with remarks indicating QR verification.

qr_scan_verified.result: QR scan proof confirms the driver is at the correct delivery point.

given: scanned QR code does not match any valid subject UUID
qr_scan_invalid.result: Proof is rejected; driver must re-scan the correct code.

given: driver attempts to complete or advance order
no proof record exists for the order or required waypoint
proof_required_gate_blocked.result: Activity transition is blocked until valid proof is submitted.

errors.message: Unable to capture QR code: the scanned code does not match the expected subject.
Proof of delivery is required before this step can be completed.
Signature data is required for signature proof capture.

events.name: order.proof_captured
events.description: Fired when a proof record is successfully created for an order or waypoint.

related.reason: Proof gates are enforced at activity transition points in the state machine.
pod_required flag on the order determines whether proof is needed.
Driver app submits proof via the capture-signature or capture-qr endpoints.

goals.description: Capture proof of pickup or drop-off for an order or specific waypoint/entity, supporting digital signature, photo upload, and QR code scan as verification methods.

constraints.description: Data consistency must be maintained across concurrent operations

tradeoffs.reason: data consistency must be maintained across all operations
